---
title: "Final Project Proposal"
author: "Vincent Chau, Christie Wong, Jimmy Leng, Arsenii Vasilev"
date: "2026-02-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Problem Statement and Motivation

- define problem & research questions
  How do outside factors delay international flights?
- explain why the problem is important and interesting
  The problem is important because international aviation contrivutes trillions of dollars to the GDP of many countires, creating economic growth in their respective countries. Furthermore, many political figures rely on flights to travel from place to place. Having an understandin in why flights may be delayed can help prevent time issues as well as potentially save lives. This topic is interesting because we are discovering potential reasons in what factors are the main factors of delays. If we were to pinpoint specific indicators, we could possibly create predictive models based off these factors to ensure timely flights and imrpove efficiency in the avion industry.   
- outline specific goals/hypotheses to be addressed
  Our goals are to clean, 
- key questions you plan to explore
  How does the weather of particular regions affect flight delays?
  How do holidays/popular vacation times affect flight delays?
  Do popular vacation locations mean an increase in flight delays?
  Do specific weather conditions and seaons delay flights more than others?
  
  


## Data Description

- identify dataset(s) to be used & cite data source/link
- highlight variables important to your research questions
- any data limitations? (ex: missing values)
- assess data cleanliness (describe cleaning plan)
- if using multiple datasets, how will you merge/integrate them?
- justify why the dataset(s) are sufficient to your research questions



## Exploratory Data Analysis (Extra Credit)

- include results from prelim EDA
- describe what insights EDA has provided for project


## Methodology & Analysis Plan

- describe analysis/modeling plan
- link methods directly to project objectives
- specify detailed techniques you plan to use (ex: visualization, modeling, evaluation methods)
- ensure methods are appropriate + thoughtfully chosen (not vague)
- include realistic work plan + timeline showing project feasibility


## Alternative Strategies & Backup Plans

- discuss potential pitfalls/challenges you might encounter
- propose clear alternative strategies + back up plans
- list 2-3 other ideas you have brainstormed, each with a couple bullet points of details
